Ad Text:
About the Company

We’re partnering with a highly capitalised, ambitious business building a completely new category: AI-native services platforms. Instead of selling software into companies, they acquire and own real-world service businesses and rebuild them with AI embedded at the core of operations.

The ambition is to transform industries that have historically been impossible to scale due to reliance on human judgement. By embedding AI directly into decision-making, operations and workflows, these businesses become compounding, software-like platforms rather than traditional services businesses.

The long-term vision is to create a platform where intelligence compounds across multiple industries, improving with every business, every system and every decision.

About the Role

As a AI Full Stack Engineer, you will be part of the small, high-calibre team building the core intelligence layer that powers this model.

This is not a feature-focused role. You will be designing and deploying systems that sit at the heart of real operating businesses, directly influencing how work gets done and how decisions are made.

You will:

Design and build end-to-end AI-native systems across frontend, backend and infrastructure
Develop LLM-powered applications including agents, orchestration layers and decision systems
Build internal tools and interfaces used daily by operators across multiple industries
Create data and feedback loops that allow systems to continuously improve
Work directly with real-world problems that are often ambiguous, messy and high impact
The output of your work will not sit in a dashboard. It will be used in live environments where performance, accuracy and usability matter.

About You

You are a true builder who thrives in environments where ownership is high and structure is limited.

You likely bring:

Strong full stack engineering capability with experience shipping products end to end
Hands-on experience applying AI in production, particularly with LLMs, agents or orchestration
The ability to think in systems, not just features
A product mindset with a focus on usability and real-world application
Comfort working directly with complex, imperfect business problem
You may come from: Early-stage or high-growth product companies
A founding or ex-founder background where you’ve built from scratch
Environments where you’ve had to move fast, make decisions and own outcomes
Most importantly, you care about building things that actually get used and deliver impact
Key Benefits

Founding impact – shape the core systems and direction of a new category from day one
Meaningful equity – genuine ownership in an early-stage platform with long-term ambition
High-calibre team – work alongside a small group of top-tier, product-minded engineers
Real-world impact – build systems tied directly to revenue, operations and decision-making
AI-first environment – no legacy constraints, building with modern AI capabilities from the ground up
Complex, interesting problems – operate in real industries where the challenges are nuanced and non-linear
Autonomy and ownership – minimal bureaucracy, high trust and the ability to move quickly

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer / Data Scientist (Azure, SQL, LLMs)
Ad Text:
Why this role stands out

This isn’t a pure data science role sitting in notebooks.

It’s a hands-on, end-to-end position where you’ll design data pipelines, optimise large-scale SQL environments, and build real-world AI features used in production.

You’ll be working on a modern Azure platform, solving complex data problems, and helping shape how advanced analytics and LLM capabilities are embedded into core products.

If you enjoy building, not just modelling, this is worth a look.

What you’ll be doing
Designing and optimising ETL pipelines in Microsoft SQL Server
Implementing incremental data processing (CDC / Change Tracking)
Building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps
Developing and deploying data science and LLM-driven features
Working across Azure services to deliver scalable, production-grade solutions
Tuning and troubleshooting complex SQL workloads
Partnering with product and engineering teams to deliver data-driven functionality
What we’re looking for
Strong experience with Microsoft SQL Server (ETL, performance tuning, CDC)
Solid background (3 plus years) across data engineering and/or data science
Experience working in Azure environments
Exposure to CI/CD (Azure DevOps preferred)
Hands-on experience with LLMs or modern AI tooling
Ability to build production-ready solutions, not just prototypes
Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset
Full working rights in Australia
Nice to have (not essential)
Databricks, OneLake or similar lakehouse technologies
Spark or distributed data processing
Geospatial / spatial data experience
Strong mathematical or statistical background
Experience in mining, resources, or heavy industry
The environment
Established, well-funded technology business
Real products, real users, real impact
Complex data challenges at scale
Collaborative engineering and product teams
Long-term opportunity with strong technical ownership
What’s on offer
Competitive salary + super
Brisbane CBD location
Flexible working (with an in-office core)
Work on modern Azure and AI-driven systems
Genuine influence over architecture and direction
If you’re someone who enjoys getting into the detail, solving hard data problems, and seeing your work in production - this is a strong opportunity.

Are you passionate about data strategy, architecture, and empowering businesses with data-driven insights? Do you enjoy designing scalable solutions and guiding organizations through complex data challenges? At InterWorks, we’re looking for a Data Architect who thrives in dynamic environments and is ready to help shape the future of data for our clients.

This role blends the rigor of enterprise data architecture and governance with the energy of consulting and solution-building. You’ll work with clients across industries to build robust data platforms, define standards, and implement governance frameworks that create lasting value.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the Melbourne, Australia InterWorks office. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications.

What You’ll Do

Architect and implement enterprise-level data solutions that align with business goals and compliance requirements.

Design and optimize data models, storage solutions, and integration pipelines across various platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, etc.).

Partner directly with clients to develop scalable data platforms, data governance frameworks, and analytics strategies.

Create and oversee ETL/ELT processes and advise on best practices for data quality, security, and accessibility.

Serve as a strategic liaison between business and IT to bridge gaps and ensure alignment.

Manage project timelines, expectations, and deliverables with clear communication and strong stakeholder engagement.

What You’ll Need

Must-Haves

5+ years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related field

High proficiency in SQL and strong data modeling skills

Experience in data governance, compliance standards, and enterprise data strategy

Excellent communication skills—written, verbal, and interpersonal

Ability to thrive in client-facing environments and lead cross-functional teams

Adaptability and problem-solving mindset in fast-paced and ambiguous situations

Proven experience with data lakehouse architecture including the storage and processing of semi-structured and unstructured file types

Proven support for data science initiatives

Understanding of pipeline and cost monitoring and optimisation

Comfortable leading business engagements to source requirements or refine data models

Experience with Azure & AWS CSP's preferable

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)

Familiarity with modern data warehouses and cloud platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks)

ETL tool experience (e.g., dbt, Matillion, Informatica, Fivetran)

Knowledge of AI/ML concepts and their role in data platforms

Degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field (preferred)

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.
